evals.name: reconstruct-from-spec-offline
evals.prompt: I'm in the cad-autoresearch repo. Reconstruct the sample_bracket part from its spec on the spec track. Read tasks/sample_bracket/spec.md, write a build123d candidate that defines a module-level `result`, and grade it. Don't read anything under ground_truth/. Show me the score.
evals.expected_output: Writes a build123d program (candidate.py) defining `result`, runs grade_one.py (or the mock loop) to grade it, reports the composite + per-layer line, optimizes in the body->bbox->volume->iou->topology->chamfer order, and never reads ground_truth/. Aims for composite >= ~0.95.

evals.name: polarity-and-contract-discipline
evals.prompt: Here's a part to model in build123d: a 50x30x10mm plate with a single 12mm-diameter hole drilled all the way through the centre. The volume keeps coming out too high when I try it. What's wrong and what's the correct candidate?
evals.expected_output: Diagnoses the polarity error (a hole that should SUBTRACT material was added or not cut) and gives a correct candidate using Hole()/Mode.SUBTRACT, defining `result`, self-contained, mm. Explains volume catches polarity errors. Respects the candidate contract (no self-export/grade).
